results.name: Meta Platforms
results.key_finding: First ADJUDICATED social-media-addiction jury verdict (K.G.M. v. Meta & YouTube, $6M, Mar 25 2026) plus a confidential Breathitt County settlement materially reinforce Meta's already-Critical floor, but a single bellwether verdict within an MDL of 2,664 unresolved cases does not move the composite below the 5-point threshold; ACC already at 1.0.

results.name: Anthropic
results.key_finding: The Jun 12 2026 Fable 5 / Mythos 5 shutdown was a US-government export-control coercion, not Anthropic misconduct (conduct-vs-coercion, net-neutral); Anthropic's pre-emptive restriction of Mythos to 50 vetted orgs is consistent with its published anticipatory-awareness profile. No new adverse conduct.

results.name: OpenAI
results.key_finding: 42-state AG subpoena (served Jun 13 2026) targeting ads, data, minors, sycophancy and safety is a pre-adjudication investigation, not a finding; no scorable movement, but flagged for monitoring if the probe produces adjudicated findings.

results.name: xAI/Grok
results.key_finding: Whistleblower wrongful-termination lawsuit (Devin Kim, filed Jun 10 2026) alleges safety-over-speed culture and references the prior 'Hitler' / NCII incidents; allegation is pre-adjudication and xAI is already at the absolute Critical floor (0). Floor reinforced.

results.name: SpaceX AI
results.key_finding: SpaceX AI is NOT present in the ai-labs index; the xAI/SpaceX merger and Kim whistleblower suit make it a candidate for addition. Flagged for index-addition review, not scoreable tonight.

results.name: Amazon
results.key_finding: NLRB joint-employer complaint (Palmdale DSP) and the Teamsters first-ever bargaining order against Amazon are consistent with the already-Critical internal-consistency/accountability profile; the NLRB complaint remains a pre-adjudication allegation. No threshold-crossing movement.

results.name: Apple
results.key_finding: A 54-member Congressional Labor Caucus letter (Jun 4 2026) calling for an NLRB investigation into Apple's closure of its unionized Towson store is a pre-adjudication advocacy/allegation; no finding yet. Confirms at published Functional score.

results.name: Bolivia
results.key_finding: The Jun 2026 'Law Regulating States of Exception' expands authority to deploy the army against protesters but is only an enabling framework; no actual deployment, deaths, or new escalation beyond the protest repression that set the near-floor 6.3. No scorable movement.

results.name: India
results.key_finding: HRW (Jun 16 2026) documents BSF unlawfully pushing ethnic Bengalis across the Bangladesh border (200+ in pushbacks since Jun 1; ~5,000 reportedly forced to leave) under a 'detect, delete, deport' policy; investigative documentation reinforces the already-Critical score but does not cross threshold.

results.name: Iran
results.key_finding: Execution surge (2,159 executions in 2025, highest since 1981; 40+ political hangings incl. 19 protesters in 2026; secret executions, torture-extracted confessions) reinforces the near-floor Critical score. No room for further scorable movement.

results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: HRW (May 19 2026) documents aid curbs and civilian killings during the ceasefire (aid trucks down from 4,200 to 590/week; 856 killed, 2,463 wounded during the ceasefire period); reinforces the Critical floor (0).

results.name: Russia
results.key_finding: Mass missile/drone strikes on Ukrainian cities (Jun 2 2026: 22 civilians killed, 138 wounded across Kyiv/Dnipro/Kharkiv/Poltava/Zaporizhzhia) plus the documented full range of violations attribute the harm to Russia; reinforces the Critical floor (0).

results.name: Myanmar
results.key_finding: Junta atrocities (30,000+ political prisoners, 2,200+ custody deaths, escalating airstrikes, 3.6M displaced; war crimes/crimes against humanity per Amnesty/HRW/Fortify Rights) reinforce the Critical floor (0).

results.name: Ukraine
results.key_finding: The highest-civilian-casualty attacks are inflicted ON Ukraine BY Russia (victim attribution to Russia, not Ukrainian state conduct); Ukraine's own compassion profile is unchanged. Confirms at published Functional score.

results.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
results.key_finding: 2026 Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak (DRC 837 cases / 196 deaths as of Jun 14; IRC estimates only ~20% of contacts located) strains a weak, conflict-disrupted response, but a WHO-backed government response exists; consistent with the published Developing-boundary score.

results.name: Cablevision/Altice
results.key_finding: Rotation/staleness backfill; no fresh material adverse evidence surfaced. Confirms at published Developing score (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Cabot Corporation
results.key_finding: Rotation/staleness backfill; no fresh 2026 material news found. Confirms at published Developing score (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Callon Petroleum
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; Callon was acquired by APA Corporation (2024) and no longer independent — stale index artifact. No fresh adverse evidence; flagged informally for catalog hygiene. Confirms at published score.

results.name: Cal Dive / Cal Dive International
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; Cal Dive / Cal Dive International are largely defunct (bankruptcy-era artifacts) and duplicated in the index. No fresh adverse evidence; flagged informally for catalog de-duplication. Confirms at published score.

errors: spacex-ai: not present in ai-labs index; cannot be scored. Flagged for index-addition review (xAI/SpaceX merger + Grok whistleblower context).
cal-dive / cal-dive-international: duplicate, likely-defunct entries in fortune-500.json; flagged for catalog de-duplication and possible removal.
callon-petroleum: acquired by APA Corporation (2024); no longer an independent entity — flagged for catalog hygiene.
notes: All 19 assessed entities confirm at their current published composites (delta 0.0). No composite delta reached the 5-point change-proposal threshold, so zero change proposals were written. Rationale: the highest-severity entities (Meta 7.8, Bolivia 6.3, Iran 2.5, and the 0-floor entities Israel/Russia/Myanmar/xAI) are already at or near their floors with no headroom for a scorable downgrade, and the freshest material developments are predominantly (a) pre-adjudication allegations/investigations (OpenAI 42-state subpoena, xAI whistleblower suit, Amazon NLRB complaint, Apple NLRB referral), (b) government coercion rather than entity conduct (Anthropic Fable/Mythos shutdown, net-neutral), or (c) victim-side harm attributable to a different actor (Ukraine casualties attributable to Russia). The one genuinely ADJUDICATED finding — the K.G.M. v. Meta jury verdict — was weighed proportionally as a single bellwether within an MDL of 2,664 unresolved cases and reinforces, but does not lower, Meta's already-Critical score. Canonical-formula reconstruction of every published baseline agreed within 0.5 points; no math-hygiene issues.
